claim: The United States deployed three aircraft carriers to the Middle East for the first time in over two decades to enhance naval capabilities amid stalled ceasefire talks.
US forces intercepted and boarded a stateless vessel linked to Iran in the context of ongoing ship interdictions.
Additional thousands of US forces are being deployed to the Middle East region.
The IDF struck Iranian regime institutions tied to internal security and social control, as well as energy infrastructure including the Shahran oil depot near Tehran.
The IDF conducted strikes on June 14-15 targeting Iranian air defenses, military airports including Mashhad Airport and Mehrabad, and radar sites.
The IDF conducted an easternmost strike on an Artesh airbase near Mashhad to establish air superiority over Iran.
Iran launched approximately 30 ballistic missiles targeting Haifa in Operation True Promise III, injuring two civilians, with the IDF intercepting approximately 92% of missiles.
Iran has conducted a total of 10 ballistic missile barrages as part of Operation True Promise III.
Supreme Leader Khamenei appointed Maj. Gen. Amir Hatami as Artesh commander, emphasizing 'active deterrence.'
IRGC gunboats fired on container ships in the Strait of Hormuz, renewing attacks despite ceasefire extensions.
IRGC commander-in-chief Ahmad Vahidi is under US consideration as a strike target if diplomatic talks collapse.
US President Trump extended a ceasefire to allow Iran time for a 'unified proposal,' with Iranian leadership response expected by Wednesday, April 29, 2026.
Iran's negotiating team in Islamabad spans a broad political spectrum to preempt hardliner opposition to ceasefire talks.
Revolutionary Guard commanders resist ceasefire talks until the Strait of Hormuz issue resolves.
Iran briefly reopened the Strait of Hormuz but reimposed a blockade on April 18, firing on a tanker.
President Pezeshkian faces backlash from hardliners for a conditional war-end offer, with diplomats like Zarif and Rouhani branded as traitors.
A Provisional Leadership Council consisting of President Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Arafi holds interim power after Israeli strikes.
Israeli strikes disrupted succession planning including a strike on the Assembly of Experts in Qom on March 3, 2026.
Significant discontent divides Iranian civilians, who favor ceasefire, from Revolutionary Guards, who oppose negotiations.
Iranian state media claims 87% public support for war amid infighting and harsh rhetoric against dissent.
Iran faces severe protests with a brutal crackdown including shoot-to-kill orders from Khamenei issued January 9, 2026.
An internet blackout continues in Iran with Starlink seizures as part of repression efforts.
The Iranian regime deployed 800 to 5,000 Iraqi fighters for repression of internal dissent.
Israeli strikes targeted Iranian repression infrastructure including intelligence facilities and Basij bases.
The Iranian regime has framed protesters as 'terrorists' as part of its repression strategy.
Supreme Leader Khamenei suffered facial injuries requiring surgery following Israeli strikes, according to reports.
The conflict between Iran and Israel has been ongoing for approximately 60 days, approaching a threshold requiring potential US congressional approval for continued action.
